expectations.text: Triggers the design gate (Phase 0) — asks clarifying questions about the auth system before decomposing
expectations.evidence: Phase 0 trigger conditions (line 144): 'The spec mentions 3+ distinct features or components' (registration, login, password reset = 3), 'The spec involves external integrations' (JWT, email, password hashing). Both conditions met. Design Refinement Process (line 158): 'Ask the user 1-3 targeted questions about ambiguities.'

expectations.text: Produces a design summary with components, data flow, and key decisions before proceeding
expectations.evidence: Phase 0 step 3 (line 168): 'present a brief design document' with template showing Components, Data Flow, Key Decisions, Out of Scope sections. Line 187: 'HARD GATE: No decomposition (Phase 2) until the design is approved.'

expectations.text: Decomposes into at least 3 work units (registration, login, password-reset or similar)
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 (line 205): 'Analyze the specification and decompose into independent work units.' The example in lines 235-248 shows exactly this pattern: user-registration, user-login, password-reset as 3 units.

expectations.text: Identifies dependencies between units (login depends on registration, password-reset depends on registration)
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 Dependency Analysis (line 218): 'Units that import/use types or functions from other units are dependent.' Example (lines 243-248): login depends on [user-registration], password-reset depends on [user-registration].

expectations.text: Respects dependency order — implements registration before login and password-reset
expectations.evidence: Execution loop respects dependency order via eager dispatch (line 468): 'All units with no dependencies are immediately ready.' Then 'Any unit whose dependencies are now ALL complete gets added to the ready queue.' Registration has no deps → runs first. Login and password-reset depend on registration → run after.

expectations.text: Each work unit goes through the full RED→GREEN→review cycle independently
expectations.evidence: Line 285: 'Unit completion requires ALL of: Test Writer → RED → Code Writer → GREEN → Spec Compliance → Adversarial → Code Quality.' Line 365: 'Review Pipeline (MANDATORY — every unit, no exceptions).' Line 431: 'This is the ONLY point where a unit may be marked complete' — after all three reviews pass. Each unit follows this pipeline independently within its concurrency slot (line 486).

expectations.text: Runs a final integration test across all units after individual completion
expectations.evidence: Phase 5 (line 499): 'IRON LAW: No completion claim without fresh verification evidence.' Step 1: 'Run the FULL test suite (all test files together) to check for integration issues.' Step 4: 'Cross-unit integration check: Do units that depend on each other actually work together?'

expectations.text: The report shows all work units with their individual phase statuses
expectations.evidence: Phase 6 (line 523): 'IRON LAW: No cleanup (Phase 7) without a report.' reference/report-format.md template shows per-unit sections with phase status table (Test Writer, RED, Code Writer, GREEN, Spec Compliance, Adversarial Review). Phase 7 pre-check (line 547) verifies report exists.

expectations.text: Assigns model and effort per agent based on complexity — opus for architecture-sensitive Test Writers and reviewers, sonnet for Code Writers
expectations.evidence: Complexity-Based Assignment (line 85): 'Architecture tasks (ambiguous spec, many deps, design-sensitive): Test Writers, reviewers, and implementers get opus/session-effort. Code Writers and quality reviewers stay sonnet/high.' Auth system with external integrations and dependencies qualifies as architecture task.

eval_feedback.overall: All expectations pass. The auth-system eval tests the design gate and dependency ordering which worked well in iteration-1's real execution. The key improvement is expectation 6 — the full review cycle — which now has three layers of enforcement.
